channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Melania Trump to debut documentary with Amazon Prime Video
item.description: Incoming first lady Melania Trump will star in a documentary film project with Amazon Prime Video set to debut in the second half of 2025, an Amazon spokesperson announced Sunday. Filming for the documentary began in December 2024 and is ongoing, according to the press release.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Latest Samsung Galaxy S25 leak includes predicted specs, pricing, and color details
item.description: Here's what we think might be coming when these phones are unveiled, which should be sometime later this month.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Find out who’s behind any phone number with this free lookup tool
item.description: I don’t know about you, but practically every time my phone rings, my heart rate starts skyrocketing.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ditch boring emoji and create your own unique versions with Genmoji on iPhone
item.description: Genmoji, the latest addition to Apple's Apple Intelligence suite, uses artificial intelligence to generate customized emoji based on text descriptions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra leak shows off the phone's super-thin bezels
item.description: A well-respected tipster has posted a shot of what looks to be the right-hand side of the Galaxy S25 Ultra.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Dating Sunday 2025: The busiest day on dating apps is almost here
item.description: Dating apps are gearing up for their busiest day of the year: Dating Sunday. This landmark day in the dating world always lands on the first Sunday of January.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: My 2025 tech resolutions: ditching X, avoiding AI, and more
item.description: So many of 2024’s tech plot twists defied predictions, but we should have seen one thing coming: The industry would once again pivot away from learning from its mistakes.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: A double dose of nasty winter is about to smack much of the US with snow, ice and biting cold
item.description: Meteorologists forecast a strong snow and ice storm followed by brutally cold conditions will soon smack the eastern two-thirds of the United States.
item.dc:creator: By Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple settles Siri eavesdropping accusations for $95M
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging the tech giant used its voice assistant Siri to obtain and share private conversations without users' permission.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Working from the office means a pay cut
item.description: In recent months, companies have been forcing workers back into the office with increasing frequency. Annoyingly, many of these are big tech businesses that can most easily enable their workers to work remotely. This trend, likely to continue in 2025, costs workers real money.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
item.description: The launch of ChatGPT in November 2022 set off a generative AI gold rush, with companies scrambling to adopt the technology and demonstrate innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Easy internet sleuth tricks you’ll wish you knew sooner
item.description: Here are some powerful online sleuthing tricks you can use to check the credibility of a website, see old pictures of places you've lived and even learn more about your neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Americans more likely to embrace driverless cars, but still skeptical: Survey
item.description: Americans are more likely to embrace driverless cars but are still skeptical of them, according to a new survey from YouGov. In the survey, 35 percent of Americans said when it comes to driverless cars’ advancement, they are “very enthusiastic” or “somewhat enthusiastic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Go teams struggle with coding standards – survey
item.description: Although developer sentiment toward the Go language remains very positive, teams face a challenge when it comes to maintaining consistent coding standards across Go code bases, according to Go developer survey results for the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The best new Windows 11 features of 2024
item.description: Microsoft is a little sneaky. Sure, there’s just one “big” update for Windows 11 each year. But Microsoft’s Windows team is always working on something, and new Windows 11 features are arriving on PCs every month — even outside of those high-profile updates.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Honey browser extension accused of scamming influencers, users
item.description: In the the 23-minute video by user MegaLag, the PayPal property is accused of abusing the affiliate link system for its own profit.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KRON4
